endpoints.name: Find US Coordinates for a Place Name
endpoints.description: Step 0 of the US weather workflow, for when you only have a place name or address rather than coordinates. Convert a US location (e.g. 'Stowe, VT', 'Denver, Colorado', or a street address) into latitude/longitude, then pass the result to points. Returns up to a few candidate matches, each with a descriptive name and coordinates already rounded to 4 decimal places (ready for points). Pick the match whose name best fits what the user meant. US locations only.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: US place name or address to look up, e.g. 'Stowe, VT', 'Denver, Colorado', or '1600 Pennsylvania Ave, Washington, DC'.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of candidate matches to return.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Response format.

parameters.name: countrycodes
parameters.description: Restrict results to the United States.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.description: Nominatim requires a User-Agent identifying the client.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Request JSON output.

response.transform: {results: [.[] | {name: .display_name, latitude: ((.lat | tonumber) * 10000 | round / 10000), longitude: ((.lon | tonumber) * 10000 | round / 10000)}]}

endpoints.name: Resolve US Coordinates to a Forecast Grid
endpoints.description: Step 1 of the US weather workflow. Convert a US latitude/longitude into the National Weather Service grid that covers it. If you only have a place name, call geocode first to obtain coordinates. Returns the office (gridId), gridX, and gridY needed by the forecast and forecast_hourly tools, plus the nearest city/state, time zone, radar station, and forecast zone. Only points within the United States and its territories are covered. Coordinates must have at most 4 decimal places.

parameters.name: latitude
parameters.description: Latitude in decimal degrees, at most 4 decimal places (e.g. 38.8894 for Washington, DC).

parameters.name: longitude
parameters.description: Longitude in decimal degrees, at most 4 decimal places (e.g. -77.0352 for Washington, DC).

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.description: NWS requires a User-Agent identifying the client.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Request GeoJSON output.

response.transform: {gridId: .properties.gridId, gridX: .properties.gridX, gridY: .properties.gridY, city: .properties.relativeLocation.properties.city, state: .properties.relativeLocation.properties.state, timeZone: .properties.timeZone, radarStation: .properties.radarStation, forecastZone: .properties.forecastZone, county: .properties.county, fireWeatherZone: .properties.fireWeatherZone}

endpoints.name: Get US 7-Day Forecast
endpoints.description: Get the official US 7-day forecast (daytime and overnight periods) for a forecast grid cell. Requires office, gridX, and gridY from the points tool. Each period includes its name (e.g. Tonight, Tuesday), temperature, wind speed/direction, chance of precipitation, and a short and detailed forecast. Use forecast_hourly for an hour-by-hour outlook.

parameters.name: office
parameters.description: NWS forecast office / grid ID from the points tool (gridId), e.g. LWX, OKX, TOP.

parameters.name: gridX
parameters.description: Grid X coordinate from the points tool.

parameters.name: gridY
parameters.description: Grid Y coordinate from the points tool.

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Unit system, fixed to SI so temperatures are in Celsius and consistent with current_conditions.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.description: NWS requires a User-Agent identifying the client.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Request GeoJSON output.

response.transform: {updated: .properties.updateTime, periods: [.properties.periods[] | {name, startTime, endTime, isDaytime, temperature, temperatureUnit, windSpeed, windDirection, precipProbability: .probabilityOfPrecipitation.value, shortForecast, detailedForecast}]}

endpoints.name: Get US Hourly Forecast
endpoints.description: Get the US hour-by-hour forecast for the NEXT 24 HOURS only for a forecast grid cell. This is the compact default; prefer it. Requires office, gridX, and gridY from the points tool. Each hour includes its start time, temperature, wind speed/direction, chance of precipitation, and a short forecast. Use forecast for the 7-day day/night outlook, or forecast_hourly_full only if you specifically need beyond 24 hours.

parameters.name: office
parameters.description: NWS forecast office / grid ID from the points tool (gridId), e.g. LWX, OKX, TOP.

parameters.name: gridX
parameters.description: Grid X coordinate from the points tool.

parameters.name: gridY
parameters.description: Grid Y coordinate from the points tool.

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Unit system, fixed to SI so temperatures are in Celsius and consistent with current_conditions.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.description: NWS requires a User-Agent identifying the client.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Request GeoJSON output.

response.transform: {updated: .properties.updateTime, periods: [.properties.periods[] | {startTime, temperature, temperatureUnit, windSpeed, windDirection, precipProbability: .probabilityOfPrecipitation.value, shortForecast}][0:24]}

endpoints.name: Get US Full Hourly Forecast (Large)
endpoints.description: Get the COMPLETE US hour-by-hour forecast (roughly 156 hours / 7 days) for a forecast grid cell. WARNING: this returns a large response and consumes significant context — call it only when you specifically need hourly detail beyond the next 24 hours. For the usual case use forecast_hourly (next 24 hours) or forecast (7-day day/night outlook). Requires office, gridX, and gridY from the points tool. Each hour includes its start time, temperature, wind speed/direction, chance of precipitation, and a short forecast.

parameters.name: office
parameters.description: NWS forecast office / grid ID from the points tool (gridId), e.g. LWX, OKX, TOP.

parameters.name: gridX
parameters.description: Grid X coordinate from the points tool.

parameters.name: gridY
parameters.description: Grid Y coordinate from the points tool.

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Unit system, fixed to SI so temperatures are in Celsius and consistent with current_conditions.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.description: NWS requires a User-Agent identifying the client.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Request GeoJSON output.

response.transform: {updated: .properties.updateTime, periods: [.properties.periods[] | {startTime, temperature, temperatureUnit, windSpeed, windDirection, precipProbability: .probabilityOfPrecipitation.value, shortForecast}]}

endpoints.name: List US Observation Stations for a Grid
endpoints.description: List National Weather Service observation stations for a forecast grid cell, nearest first. Requires office, gridX, and gridY from the points tool. Returns each station's identifier and name; pass a station identifier to current_conditions to read its latest observation.

parameters.name: office
parameters.description: NWS forecast office / grid ID from the points tool (gridId), e.g. LWX, OKX, TOP.

parameters.name: gridX
parameters.description: Grid X coordinate from the points tool.

parameters.name: gridY
parameters.description: Grid Y coordinate from the points tool.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.description: NWS requires a User-Agent identifying the client.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Request GeoJSON output.

endpoints.name: Get US Current Conditions
endpoints.description: Get the latest observed weather conditions from a National Weather Service station (use the stations tool to find a station identifier near a location). Returns observation time, a text description, temperature, dewpoint, relative humidity, wind direction/speed/gust, barometric pressure, visibility, and heat index / wind chill when present. Observation values are reported in SI units (degrees Celsius, metres per second, pascals, percent).

parameters.name: stationId
parameters.description: NWS station identifier from the stations tool, e.g. KDCA, KOKC, KSEA.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.description: NWS requires a User-Agent identifying the client.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Request GeoJSON output.

response.transform: {timestamp: .properties.timestamp, description: .properties.textDescription, temperature: .properties.temperature, dewpoint: .properties.dewpoint, relativeHumidity: .properties.relativeHumidity, windDirection: .properties.windDirection, windSpeed: .properties.windSpeed, windGust: .properties.windGust, barometricPressure: .properties.barometricPressure, visibility: .properties.visibility, heatIndex: .properties.heatIndex, windChill: .properties.windChill}

endpoints.name: Get Active US Weather Alerts
endpoints.description: Get active National Weather Service watches, warnings, and advisories. Narrow results with a two-letter US state code (area, e.g. KS) or a point as 'latitude,longitude'. With no filter this returns all active alerts across the United States, which can be large. Each alert is summarized to its event type, severity, urgency, affected area, headline, and expiry time (up to 50 alerts); the headline conveys the key details.

parameters.name: area
parameters.description: Two-letter US state or territory code to filter alerts, e.g. KS, CA, TX, PR.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: Filter alerts to those covering a single point, given as 'latitude,longitude' (at most 4 decimal places each), e.g. 38.8894,-77.0352.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Optional severity filter.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.description: NWS requires a User-Agent identifying the client.

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Request GeoJSON output.

response.transform: {count: (.features | length), alerts: [.features[] | {event: .properties.event, severity: .properties.severity, urgency: .properties.urgency, areaDesc: .properties.areaDesc, headline: .properties.headline, expires: .properties.expires}][0:50]}
